description: LogCat Viewer - Add a bottom-sheet panel in CodeProjectActivity showing live logcat output filtered by the app's package name, with color-coded log levels, clear button, and auto-scroll.
steps: Create new layout file app/src/main/res/layout/layout_logcat_panel.xml: A LinearLayout (vertical) containing: (1) a horizontal toolbar with a 'Logcat' title TextView, a clear button (ImageButton with ic_delete or similar), and a close button; (2) a RecyclerView for log lines that fills the remaining space. Use Material Design colors.

Create new Java class app/src/main/java/pro/sketchware/codeproject/ui/LogcatPanel.java: This is a helper class that manages logcat reading. Fields: Process logcatProcess, Thread readerThread, List<LogEntry> entries, RecyclerView recyclerView, LogcatAdapter adapter, String packageFilter, boolean autoScroll=true, boolean isRunning. Inner class LogEntry with fields: String level, String tag, String message, String raw.

In LogcatPanel.java: Method `start(String packageName)` - Executes `Runtime.getRuntime().exec(new String[]{"logcat", "-v", "brief", "--pid=" + getPidForPackage(packageName)})` or simply `logcat` with grep-style filtering. Since we cannot guarantee the built app is running, use a simpler approach: `Runtime.getRuntime().exec(new String[]{"logcat", "-v", "tag"})` and filter lines containing the package name in the reader thread.
In LogcatPanel.java: Method `stop()` - destroys the logcat process and interrupts the reader thread. Method `clear()` - clears the entries list and notifies adapter.

Create app/src/main/java/pro/sketchware/codeproject/ui/LogcatAdapter.java: A RecyclerView.Adapter that displays log lines. Each item is a single-line TextView with monospace font. Color the text based on log level: V=gray, D=blue (#2196F3), I=green (#4CAF50), W=orange (#FF9800), E=red (#F44336). Parse the log level from the first character of the line (V/, D/, I/, W/, E/).

Create item layout app/src/main/res/layout/item_logcat_line.xml: A simple TextView with monospace font (fontFamily='monospace'), textSize 11sp, single line with ellipsize end, padding 2dp horizontal/1dp vertical.

Modify app/src/main/res/layout/activity_code_project.xml: Wrap the existing LinearLayout content area in a vertical LinearLayout or use a weighted layout. Add a FrameLayout at the bottom (id=logcat_container, height=0dp, visibility=gone) that will host the logcat panel. When shown, give it layout_weight or fixed height (200dp).

Modify app/src/main/res/menu/menu_code_project.xml: Add a new menu item `<item android:id="@+id/action_logcat" android:title="@string/code_project_logcat" app:showAsAction="never" />` after the redo item.
Add string resources in app/src/main/res/values/strings.xml: `code_project_logcat` ('Logcat'), `code_project_logcat_clear` ('Clear log').

Modify CodeProjectActivity.java: Add a field `private LogcatPanel logcatPanel;`. In `setupEditor()` or a new `setupLogcat()` method, inflate the logcat panel layout into the logcat_container FrameLayout, create a LogcatPanel instance, set up the clear and close button listeners.
In CodeProjectActivity.java `onMenuItemClick()`: Handle R.id.action_logcat - toggle the logcat panel visibility. If showing, start logcat with project.getPackageName(). If hiding, stop it.
In CodeProjectActivity.java `onBackPressed()` or `onDestroy()`: Stop the logcat process to prevent resource leaks.
acceptance_criteria: A 'Logcat' menu item appears in the toolbar overflow menu
Tapping 'Logcat' toggles a panel at the bottom of the screen
The panel shows logcat output with color-coded log levels (V=gray, D=blue, I=green, W=orange, E=red)
A clear button empties the log display
The panel auto-scrolls to show the latest log line
Stopping/closing the panel terminates the logcat process
verification: Run ./gradlew assembleDebug in the project root and confirm it completes without compilation errors
findings: Build compiles cleanly. Used inline logcat panel layout in activity_code_project.xml instead of a separate include file for simplicity with ViewBinding. Used android system drawables (ic_menu_delete, ic_menu_close_clear_cancel) for the clear and close buttons. BaseAppCompatActivity.onDestroy() is public, not protected.
